article_title: Trump Expected to Nominate FCC Counsel Adam Candeub as DOJ Antitrust Chief, Filling a Vacancy Left by Omeed Assefi's Departure
summary: Submission approvable with 2 minor warning(s) — file a corrections record on merge

editor_notes.content_quality: Clear, well-structured News piece (743 words, within the 400-1200 News range). Sectioned into Overview, What We Know, A Second Leadership Change This Year, Why the Timing Matters, and What We Don't Know. The framing is consistently conditional ('plans to nominate', 'would fill', 'is being considered', 'if nominated') and correctly reflects that this is a reported/expected nomination, not a confirmed appointment. The closing 'What We Don't Know' section appropriately flags that no formal announcement has been made and the Senate timeline is unsettled.
editor_notes.source_verification: Read all 5 saved snapshots from disk. source-0 (CBS News, HTTP 200): confirms Candeub considered for assistant AG for Antitrust Division, FCC general counsel under Chairman Brendan Carr, social-media crackdown effort in Trump's first term, Assefi departing next month for family reasons, and that Slater 'was terminated in February.' source-1 (PYMNTS, HTTP 200): confirms expected nomination per Bloomberg, FCC general counsel role, Commerce/DOJ service in first term, Michigan State professor, FTC chapter of Project 2025, Trump recently met with Candeub, Assefi leaving later this month. source-3 (The Capitol Forum, HTTP 200): confirms 'emerged as a leading candidate to be nominated to lead DOJ's antitrust division', the Cella/Murray interviews quote, the 'focused largely on telecommunications and lacks direct antitrust enforcement experience' and 'atypical choice' quotes, MSU 2004, Project 2025 FTC section, paternity leave in June not expected to return. source-4 (TechCrunch, HTTP 200): confirms vocal Section 230 critic, Commerce deputy assistant secretary 2019 and acting assistant secretary, DOJ deputy associate AG late 2020, MSU law faculty 2004. source-5 (9to5Mac, HTTP 200): confirms the 'Google gets to keep paying Apple to be the default (albeit not exclusive) search engine on Safari' quote, the 12-month default limit / exclusivity-adjacent incentives language, and the DOJ-and-states cross-appeal of the final judgment 'entered on December 5, 2025' to the D.C. Circuit. The investing.com source (manifest file: null, HTTP 403 bot-blocked) could NOT be snapshotted; I did not re-fetch it. Every claim the article attributes to Investing.com/Bloomberg that is load-bearing is independently corroborated by another snapshot (Trump meeting -> PYMNTS; Project 2025 -> PYMNTS and Capitol Forum; Assefi acting since February and Slater's exit -> CBS and Capitol Forum). Two subordinate claims attributed to Investing.com appear only there: the Visa enforcement reference and the 'could take several months' confirmation estimate. Neither is in the headline/summary/lead and both are explicitly attributed; they are non-blocking.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Names verified verbatim: 'Candeub' and 'Assefi' match all snapshots. Quotes verified: 4 Capitol Forum quotes ('emerged as a leading candidate...', 'focused largely on telecommunications and lacks direct antitrust enforcement experience', 'an atypical choice to lead the antitrust division', 'were interviewed recently at the White House for the position') and the 9to5Mac Safari-default quote are all verbatim against the snapshots. The December 5, 2025 final-judgment date is verbatim in 9to5Mac. The agent correctly OMITTED the conflicting state-count figures (the 9to5Mac cross-appeal notice lists 14 named states; the article neutrally writes 'a group of states') and correctly did NOT use the '$20b' Apple figure (which appears in 9to5Mac only inside a related-article URL slug, not as a stated fact). One quote-accuracy issue: the article presents inside quotation marks the Capitol Forum phrase 'lost its Senate-confirmed head Gail Slater in February', but the snapshot's actual wording is 'lost its Senate-confirmed antitrust head, Gail Slater, when the White House ousted her in February.' The article dropped 'antitrust' and truncated the sentence before 'when the White House ousted her.' The substance (Slater lost the post in February) is accurate; the text inside the quote marks is a compression rather than a verbatim reproduction. This is the single recoverable issue that triggers APPROVE_WITH_CORRECTIONS.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional throughout, which is the central test for this political-nomination story. The piece reports the expected nomination without endorsing or attacking it, attributes every characterization (e.g., 'atypical choice', 'lacks direct antitrust enforcement experience') to The Capitol Forum rather than asserting it editorially, and handles the contested Slater departure carefully: sources conflict (CBS 'terminated', Capitol Forum 'ousted'), and the article uses the neutral 'left the role' attributed to Investing.com/Bloomberg while still surfacing the Capitol Forum framing. No loaded language, no sensationalism, no AI self-reference. The conditional framing ('expected', 'would', 'if nominated') is maintained from headline to body, correctly avoiding any implication that the nomination is confirmed.
editor_notes.originality: No prior coverage of Candeub, the Antitrust Division leadership transition, or Gail Slater's departure found in the article archive. New topic.
concerns: The Capitol Forum quote 'lost its Senate-confirmed head Gail Slater in February' is presented inside quotation marks but is a compression of the snapshot's actual wording ('lost its Senate-confirmed antitrust head, Gail Slater, when the White House ousted her in February'). Filed as a clarification correction. The substance is accurate; the quote is not verbatim.

The investing.com source (HTTP 403, bot-blocked) could not be snapshotted. Its load-bearing claims are independently corroborated by other snapshots; the Visa enforcement reference and the 'several months' confirmation estimate are sourced solely to it but are subordinate, explicitly attributed, and not in the headline/lead.
recommendations: Allowlist warning is non-blocking: thecapitolforum.com is not in config/source_allowlist.txt. The maintainer may consider adding it; the allowlist file was not modified as part of this review.
Where practical, prefer reproducing source quotes verbatim or paraphrasing outside quotation marks rather than compressing a quoted sentence.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Substantively strong, neutral, well-sourced reported-nomination story. The headline, summary, and lead are independently corroborated by CBS, PYMNTS, and The Capitol Forum; all integrity checks pass; no orphan URLs (body URLs match the sources array exactly); no bot-blocked source carries a sole load-bearing headline claim. The one recoverable issue is a single quotation-mark compression of a Capitol Forum sentence, honestly summarizable in a corrections note. Verdict: APPROVE_WITH_CORRECTIONS.

content_preview.title: Trump Expected to Nominate FCC Counsel Adam Candeub as DOJ Antitrust Chief, Filling a Vacancy Left by Omeed Assefi's Departure
content_preview.summary: The White House plans to nominate FCC General Counsel Adam Candeub to lead the DOJ Antitrust Division as acting chief Omeed Assefi prepares to leave, the second leadership turnover since February.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

The White House plans to nominate Adam Candeub, the general counsel at the Federal Communications Commission, to lead the U.S. Department of Justice's Antitrust Division, according to [PYMNTS](https://www.pymnts.com/cpi-posts/trump-expected-to-nominate-fcc-lawyer-adam-candeub-to-lead-doj-antitrust-division/). The move would fill a leadership gap created as the division's acting chief, Omeed Assefi, prepares to step down, leaving the agency that polices the country's largest technolo...

recommendations: Before merging, the human reviewer should write a corrections file at src/content/corrections/<YYYY-MM>/<article-slug>.json documenting the warning(s).
Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt
1 source(s) blocked bot access — sources likely valid but not archivable automatically
